---
title: ""
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-banner">
  <h1>About the HF Data Portal</h1>
  <p>Background, goals, and the team behind the Portal</p>
</div>
```

## What Is the HF Data Portal?

The Health Facility Data Portal is a centralised, openly accessible resource providing
standardised health facility information for malaria-endemic countries in sub-Saharan
Africa. It was developed to remove a persistent bottleneck in infectious-disease
modelling: the time-consuming, error-prone work of locating, cleaning, and harmonising
national facility lists before any analysis can begin.

The Portal currently covers **Tanzania**, **Kenya**, and **Uganda**, with additional
countries planned on the [roadmap](roadmap.html).

---

## Why Health Facility Data?

Health facility location and capacity underpin a wide range of analytical needs:

- **Transmission modelling** — estimating care-seeking rates and treatment coverage
- **Access mapping** — computing travel-time surfaces to the nearest facility
- **Intervention planning** — targeting IRS and bed-net distribution campaigns
- **Health system evaluation** — monitoring facility density and equity over time

Despite their importance, national master facility lists are rarely harmonised across
sources or years, limiting reproducibility and cross-country comparison.

---

## Our Approach

```{=html}
<div class="method-card">
  <h3>🗂 Source Collation</h3>
  <p>We draw on national Ministry of Health master facility lists, DHIS2 organisation
  unit exports, WHO SARA assessments, and curated partner datasets.</p>
</div>

<div class="method-card">
  <h3>🔗 Harmonisation</h3>
  <p>All sources are mapped to a common schema: unique facility ID, name, admin
  hierarchy (levels 1–4), facility type, ownership, GPS coordinates, and operational
  status.</p>
</div>

<div class="method-card">
  <h3>✅ Quality Assessment</h3>
  <p>Each dataset receives a structured quality report covering coordinate validity,
  duplicate records, admin code consistency, and temporal completeness. Scores are
  published alongside the data.</p>
</div>

<div class="method-card">
  <h3>📦 Versioned Release</h3>
  <p>Datasets follow semantic versioning and are archived with a DOI on Zenodo. A
  full changelog is maintained so users can track what changed between versions.</p>
</div>
```

---

## Team

The Portal is maintained by a cross-institutional team with expertise in health
data science, GIS, and malaria epidemiology.

| Name | Role | Institution |
|---|---|---|
| *Coordinator TBC* | Data Lead | — |
| *Coordinator TBC* | GIS Analysis | — |
| *Coordinator TBC* | Software & Infra | — |

*Interested in contributing? See the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/myalla-christina/hf-data-portal).*

---

## Funding & Acknowledgements

This work is supported by the **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation**. We thank the
Ministries of Health in Tanzania, Kenya, and Uganda for sharing national facility
data, and the broader open-data community whose tools make this Portal possible.

```{=html}
<a href="methodology.html" class="btn-hf-primary" style="margin-top:1.2rem; display:inline-block;">
  Read the Methods →
</a>
```
